message:
feat(ratings): add filter by minimum rating

Implements minimum rating filter for the Ratings & Reviews EPIC:
- Extended useFilterState with minRating option (URL: ?minRating=50|60|70|80|90)
- Created RatingFilter component for minimum rating dropdown
- Applied filtering logic to exclude prompts below threshold
- Updated results description to show filter info

Part of jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9 (Ratings & Reviews EPIC)
Closes bd-2ka7

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch: @@ -3,7 +3,7 @@ {"id":"bd-1d6d","title":"Remove ultrathink instruction from prompt text","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T02:22:53.539441606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T02:30:42.663176238Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T02:30:42.662998253Z","close_reason":"Removed ultrathink instructions from prompt content and exports","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1eho","title":"Feature: Auto-Tagging + Duplicate Detection (Metadata Assistant)","description":"## Background As the prompt library grows, manual tagging becomes inconsistent and duplicates sneak in. Pro users need fast, reliable metadata and dedupe support. ## Scope - Auto-suggest tags, categories, and short descriptions using deterministic rules + semantic similarity (no SKILL.md). - Duplicate/near-duplicate detection with similarity thresholds and preview diffs. - Merge flow: mark as alias, merge tags, or ignore with rationale. - CLI: `jfp tags suggest ` and `jfp dedupe scan` (Pro-only). ## Deliverables - Metadata suggestion service + similarity index. - UI panel for suggestions and duplicate resolution. - CLI scan output with JSON option. - Admin override for global tag mappings. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Similarity scoring thresholds and tag suggestion rules. - Duplicate detection determinism. ### Integration Tests - Suggestions saved to prompt metadata and reflected in search. - Dedupe merge updates references (packs/workflows/collections). ### E2E Tests - Create prompt -> receive suggestions -> accept -> verify tags. - Detect duplicate -> merge -> verify references updated. ## Success Criteria - Tag consistency improves without manual rework. - Duplicate prompts are surfaced and resolvable. - Search quality improves for Pro users.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoFinch","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:48.758255166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:34:31.681132887Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:34:31.681111768Z","close_reason":"Admin page","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1ppu","title":"Backend: Budget persistence + hard stop enforcement (Premium)","description":"Implement premium backend storage + enforcement for budget settings. Scope: API endpoints to read/write budget caps + alerts, persist per-user settings, enforce hard-stop on overages for prompt runs, and expose audit log for alerts. Mirror bead in premium repo and link cross-repo dependencies.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:04:43.856815347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:24:14.522985738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Draft backend spec for budgets/hard stop (premium repo): Data model: - budgets: user_id (pk), monthly_cap_usd, per_run_cap_usd, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled, created_at, updated_at. - budget_alerts: id, user_id, alert_type (per_run|monthly|hard_stop), cap_usd, total_cost_usd, currency, prompt_id, prompt_title, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, source (estimate|actual), created_at. - usage_rollups: user_id, month (YYYY-MM), total_cost_usd, total_tokens, last_run_at. API (premium): - GET /cli/budgets -> { budgets, usage_month, usage, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled }. - PUT /cli/budgets -> update caps/toggles; return saved payload. - GET /cli/budget-alerts?limit=&before= -> list alerts for UI/CLI. - POST /cli/budget-alerts -> optional client-side logging for cost estimates (optional). Enforcement: - On any prompt run/execute endpoint, compute projected cost. If hard_stop_enabled and projected monthly total > cap -> return 402/403 with code budget_exceeded and include caps + current usage. - If per_run cap exceeded, allow run but record alert (and return warning in response). - Monthly usage should be computed from actual usage only (estimates should not increment usage). Client integration notes (public repo): - CLI currently logs alerts locally to ~/.config/jfp/budget-alerts.jsonl. - Once backend exists, update CLI status to pull budgets/usage from /cli/budgets for premium users; local config remains fallback. - Web budget settings page is local-only; wire to backend when endpoints exist.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:03Z"},{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional spec details: Response shapes: - GET /cli/budgets -> { ok: true, data: { budgets: { monthlyCapUsd, perRunCapUsd, alertsEnabled, hardStopEnabled }, usage: { month, totalCostUsd, totalTokens, lastRunAt } } } - PUT /cli/budgets (body: partial budgets) -> same payload as GET. - Error (budget hard stop): status 402 or 403 with { ok:false, error:{ code:"budget_exceeded", message, caps:{ monthlyCapUsd, perRunCapUsd }, usage:{ month, totalCostUsd }, projected:{ totalCostUsd, inputTokens, outputTokens, model } } }. - Warning only (per-run over cap, hard stop disabled): include { warning:{ code:"budget_warning", message, capUsd, totalCostUsd } } in normal response. Rollups: - usage_rollups should be updated on actual billable runs only. - Use UTC month boundaries; month stored as YYYY-MM string for deterministic queries. Client expectations: - CLI/web should treat missing /cli/budgets endpoint as "not available" and keep local-only UI/alerts. - CLI can show budget status in `jfp status` only when endpoint returns data; otherwise fallback to config-only summary.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:17:00Z"},{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"bv triage 2026-02-01: all open beads already in_progress; continuing bd-1ppu. Remaining work is premium-repo implementation (endpoints + persistence + enforcement). Blocked here until premium repo mirror/assignment. Suggested next steps: mirror bead in premium repo; implement /cli/budgets + /cli/budget-alerts; enforce hard-stop on run endpoints; add usage rollups + tests.","created_at":"2026-02-01T22:57:22Z"},{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"bv triage 2026-02-01T23:23Z: top picks unchanged; all open beads already in_progress. Continuing bd-1ppu. Concrete next steps (premium repo): 1) Add budgets table + budget_alerts + usage_rollups (UTC month key). 2) Implement GET/PUT /cli/budgets (auth + validate, return usage). 3) Implement GET /cli/budget-alerts (paging by before/limit). 4) Enforce hard stop in run/execute endpoints; return error code budget_exceeded with caps + usage + projected cost. 5) Add warning payload for per-run overages when hard stop disabled. 6) Tests: budget validation, rollups increment on actual usage, hard-stop block, warning path. Public repo follow-up (after premium endpoints exist): - Update CLI status to prefer /cli/budgets (fallback to local config). - Wire web Budget Settings page to backend instead of localStorage; keep local-only fallback if endpoint missing.","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:24:14Z"}]} {"id":"bd-1qv8","title":"Security: Admin role must be server-derived","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-02T02:25:20.229414377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:41:07.985256805Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:41:07.985229053Z","close_reason":"Admin role now derived from JFP_ADMIN_ROLE env var instead of client-supplied x-jfp-admin-role header. Prevents privilege escalation. Updated layout.tsx, metadata page, and e2e tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1u52","title":"Bug: handle invalid tool_result content + share link by extracting rate limiting to modular lib/rate-limit module with documentation and Upstash upgrade path","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-2txr","title":"E2E Tests: History UI Components","description":"## Task Add UI-level E2E tests for the Recently Viewed History feature. ## Context The API-level E2E tests exist (e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts) but UI component tests are missing. ## Test Scenarios 1. **RecentlyViewedSidebar Component** - Sidebar displays recent items - Clear history button works - Items link to correct pages - Empty state displays correctly - Loading state shows correctly 2. **History Page (/history)** - Page loads and displays items - Search/filter works - Clear all history works - Timestamps display correctly - Links navigate correctly 3. **History Tracking Integration** - Viewing a prompt adds to history - Search queries are tracked - Deduplication prevents rapid duplicates - Items appear in sidebar after viewing ## Files to Modify - e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts (add UI tests) - e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts (add UI selectors) ## Success Criteria - All sidebar interactions tested - History page fully tested - Integration with tracking verified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralStar","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:45:40.323914674Z","created_by":"CoralStar","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005730906Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005649042Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-2txr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete: - Added 16 new UI tests (34 total including API tests) - History Page UI tests: header, empty state, items display, search filter, clear button, navigation - RecentlyViewedSidebar tests: title, empty state, history link, items display, 8-item limit - History Tracking Integration tests: cross-page sync, clear sync, search query display - Updated history-helpers.ts with better UI selectors for sidebar and history page - 68 total tests (34 × 2 browser configs: chromium + Mobile Chrome)","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:39Z"}]} {"id":"bd-2vsu","title":"Feature: Personalized Recommendations Feed (Pro)","description":"## Background Pro users want to discover high‑value prompts quickly without browsing the entire catalog. A personalized feed drives engagement and retention. ## Scope - "For You" feed based on usage signals (views, runs, saves) + explicit preferences. - Explanations: "Because you used X" or "Trending in your category". - Weekly digest email (opt‑in) with top recommendations. - CLI: `jfp recommend` returns a ranked list with rationale. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Ranking pipeline and data model for signals. - Feed UI with filters (timeframe, category). - Digest email template + scheduling job. - CLI command with JSON output. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Ranking weight calculations and deterministic ordering. - Preference filtering and exclusion rules. ### Integration Tests - Signal ingestion -> feed updates -> UI reflects changes. - Digest job generates consistent results. ### E2E Tests - Toggle preferences -> feed updates -> digest preview. - CLI recommend -> matches feed top items. ## Success Criteria - Users find relevant prompts faster. - Feed is explainable and feels trustworthy. - Opt‑in privacy settings are respected.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:02.330248162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:30:55.979101810Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} @@ -170,7 +171,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","title":"Add --json flag support for all new CLI commands","description":"Add --json flag support to all new CLI commands for machine-readable output. Commands to Update: - jfp login (output: { authenticated: true, email, tier }) - jfp logout (output: { logged_out: true }) - jfp whoami (output: { email, tier, user_id }) - jfp collections list (output: { collections: [...] }) - jfp collections show (output: { collection, items: [...] }) - jfp notes (output: { prompt_id, notes: [...] }) - jfp save (output: { saved: true, prompt_id }) - jfp list --mine/--saved (output: { prompts: [...] }) Implementation: - Add --json/-j flag to all relevant commands - When --json is set, output only valid JSON to stdout - Errors should also be JSON: { error: true, message, code } - Progress/status messages go to stderr when --json is enabled - Ensure consistent field naming across all commands ","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:15.385023105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:20:01.044458985Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:20:01.044458985Z","close_reason":"All CLI commands now have --json flag support with consistent output format","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9g76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:19.154441027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cgj5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:08.868781233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","title":"Redesign 'Clear All Filters' as a proper button","description":"# Task: Redesign Clear Filters Button ## Current Implementation (page.tsx:165-172) ```tsx {hasActiveFilters && ( )} ``` ## Problems 1. Plain text link - low visual prominence 2. Uses indigo (inconsistent with neutral design system) 3. Only visible when filters active (no affordance) 4. No icon - relies entirely on text ## Proposed Design ### Option A: Always-visible ghost button (Recommended) ```tsx ``` ### Option B: Only when active, but as a proper button ```tsx {hasActiveFilters && ( )} ``` ## Implementation Details ### Add filter count calculation ```tsx const filterCount = useMemo(() => { let count = 0; if (filters.query) count++; if (filters.category) count++; count += filters.tags.length; return count; }, [filters]); ``` ### Add accessibility ```tsx aria-label={`Clear all ${filterCount} active filters`} ``` ## Design Rationale - Ghost button maintains visual hierarchy (filters are secondary to results) - Always visible = discoverable (disabled state indicates "nothing to clear") - Icon + text = clear meaning - Count badge = precise feedback - Matches CategoryFilter/TagFilter clear button pattern ## Testing 1. With no filters: button disabled, grayed out 2. With 1 filter: button enabled, shows "(1)" 3. With multiple: button shows count 4. Click clears all and disables button 5. Keyboard accessible (Tab, Enter) ## Files Modified - apps/web/src/app/page.tsx ## Dependencies - Should use the updated neutral color palette (do after color fix) - Matches pattern already used in TagFilter.tsx (Clear button)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:38:18.479565228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T15:06:14.296451909Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T15:06:14.296451909Z","close_reason":"Implementation verified complete: Button component with ghost variant, X icon, filterCount memo, aria-label, and neutral colors. Build passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ogel","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:43:15.125413399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","title":"Feature: GitHub Actions E2E Integration","description":"Add E2E tests to GitHub Actions CI workflow. Configure CLI E2E tests to run on push/PR. Configure Playwright for web E2E with proper browser setup. Upload test artifacts (screenshots, videos, logs) on failure. Optional: multi-browser test matrix for comprehensive coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:43:26.454174609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:16:26.265863560Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T00:16:26.265863560Z","close_reason":"CI infrastructure implemented in parent EPIC","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:53:36.860791733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-jxd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:53:26.008125350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-nfm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:53:59.405040418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","title":"EPIC: Ratings & Reviews System","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-cdp. ## Overview Ratings and reviews provide quality signals for community content, helping users discover the best prompts, packs, and skills while rewarding creators for quality contributions. ## Rating System Design ### Simple Rating (MVP) - Thumbs up / thumbs down (binary) - Easy, low-friction interaction - Clear positive/negative signal - Calculate "approval rate" percentage ### Extended Rating (V2) - 1-5 star rating - Written review (optional) - Helpful/not helpful on reviews - Response from author ## Key Features ### Rate Content - One-click rating on any ratable content - Rate: prompts, packs, skills - Change rating anytime - See your rating history ### View Ratings - Show rating on content cards - Rating breakdown on detail pages - Sort by rating in lists - Filter by minimum rating ### Reviews (V2) - Write text review with rating - Edit/delete own reviews - Report inappropriate reviews - Author can respond to reviews ### Leaderboards - Top rated prompts this week/month - Top contributors by ratings received - Rising stars (new content with high ratings) ## Anti-Gaming Measures - One rating per user per item - Rate limiting on rapid ratings - Flag suspicious patterns - Weight by user reputation ## Database Schema - ratings (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_value, created_at, updated_at) - reviews (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_id, content, created_at) - review_votes (review_id, user_id, is_helpful) ## UI Components - RatingButton (thumbs up/down) - RatingDisplay (show current rating) - RatingBreakdown (detailed stats) - ReviewList (paginated reviews) - ReviewForm (write review) ## Integration Points - Community prompts (Swap Meet) - Prompt packs - Skills - Collections (V2) ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"SilverPine","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:20:02.532432863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:08:38.661920536Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Code review by agent: MVP implementation is complete and solid. Current state: - RatingButton: Thumbs up/down with haptic feedback, animations, accessible - RatingDisplay: Compact/detailed variants with approval bar - API route: /api/ratings with GET/POST, rate limiting - Rating store: In-memory with proper key generation - useRating hook: SWR pattern with optimistic updates - E2E helpers: ratings-helpers.ts for testing Remaining work per EPIC: 1. Sort by rating in prompt lists 2. Filter by minimum rating 3. Leaderboards (top rated prompts) 4. V2: Written reviews 5. V2: Author responses Note: Rating persistence requires premium repo DB.","created_at":"2026-02-03T06:08:38Z"}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","title":"EPIC: Ratings & Reviews System","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-cdp. ## Overview Ratings and reviews provide quality signals for community content, helping users discover the best prompts, packs, and skills while rewarding creators for quality contributions. ## Rating System Design ### Simple Rating (MVP) - Thumbs up / thumbs down (binary) - Easy, low-friction interaction - Clear positive/negative signal - Calculate "approval rate" percentage ### Extended Rating (V2) - 1-5 star rating - Written review (optional) - Helpful/not helpful on reviews - Response from author ## Key Features ### Rate Content - One-click rating on any ratable content - Rate: prompts, packs, skills - Change rating anytime - See your rating history ### View Ratings - Show rating on content cards - Rating breakdown on detail pages - Sort by rating in lists - Filter by minimum rating ### Reviews (V2) - Write text review with rating - Edit/delete own reviews - Report inappropriate reviews - Author can respond to reviews ### Leaderboards - Top rated prompts this week/month - Top contributors by ratings received - Rising stars (new content with high ratings) ## Anti-Gaming Measures - One rating per user per item - Rate limiting on rapid ratings - Flag suspicious patterns - Weight by user reputation ## Database Schema - ratings (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_value, created_at, updated_at) - reviews (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_id, content, created_at) - review_votes (review_id, user_id, is_helpful) ## UI Components - RatingButton (thumbs up/down) - RatingDisplay (show current rating) - RatingBreakdown (detailed stats) - ReviewList (paginated reviews) - ReviewForm (write review) ## Integration Points - Community prompts (Swap Meet) - Prompt packs - Skills - Collections (V2) ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"SilverPine","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:20:02.532432863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:27:51.556092437Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Code review by agent: MVP implementation is complete and solid. Current state: - RatingButton: Thumbs up/down with haptic feedback, animations, accessible - RatingDisplay: Compact/detailed variants with approval bar - API route: /api/ratings with GET/POST, rate limiting - Rating store: In-memory with proper key generation - useRating hook: SWR pattern with optimistic updates - E2E helpers: ratings-helpers.ts for testing Remaining work per EPIC: 1.
